0.0.1.description:
Initial release of an alpha - **working up to Chapter 2** without issue, Chapter 3 with issue. You can technically set it to any chapter goal and it should work. Untested past Chapter 3 at this time.

**Logic issues currently exist**, overworld checks past Chapter 3 do not have logic pathing, so the world will still place items there, but it shouldn't place any important progression(I hope, not fully tested)

1. Download the .apworld and double click it to install into your Archipelago location.
2. Download BepInEx-Unity.IL2CPP-win-x64 and put into your WitchSpring R game folder.
I tested this specifically with BepInEx 6.0.0-be.755 (Unity IL2CPP x64)
3. Download WSR_Plugin.zip and either replace the plugins folder in the new BepInEx folder with this one, or move the .dll from WSR_Plugin into the plugins folder. 

Launch the WitchSpring R Client and connect to your slot **BEFORE** launching the WitchSpring R game.

0.1.0.description:
Logic is better and number of checks now vary based on goal!

**I do not recommend setting a goal for anything above Chapter 3. Chapter 2 is tested and functions, Chapter 3 is half tested and mostly functions! Anything past that is at your own risk.**

1. Install [BepInEx Unity IL2CPP](https://builds.bepinex.dev/projects/bepinex_be) into the [WitchSpring R](https://store.steampowered.com/app/1958220/WitchSpring_R/) game folder
   ( Tested with [BepInEx 6.0.0-be.755](https://builds.bepinex.dev/projects/bepinex_be/755/BepInEx-Unity.IL2CPP-win-x64-6.0.0-be.755%2B3fab71a.zip) ) 
2. Launch the game once for the folders to be created
3. Place the WitchSpringRArchipelago.dll into the new BepInEx/plugins folder / Or replace the plugins folder with the one from the .zip
4. Install the .apworld - by default you should be able to double click this for your Archipelago setup to automatically place it. Otherwise, it goes into the custom worlds folder.

0.1.1.description:
## Changelog
- Instead of the plugin looking at player inventory and granting items that don't exist. There is now a new processed_received_index.txt generated to keep track of what has been granted to the player. This helps to make sure you don't have infinite consumables and don't get constantly spammed with "Duplicate equipment" warning locking up other cutscenes if there was an error granting an item in a scene.
- Please note that in your AppData > Local > Archipelago there is a WitchspringR folder that keeps track of your various game seeds and logs items sent/received
- Fixed Pudding Cave 2 to be a map that allows items to be granted on.

0.1.2.description:
Selecting a Chapter 3 goal is much less scary now and should be better! Still expect to run into bugs and game locks requiring an Alt+F4. Please save often!
Reminder the goals are what Chapter you need to get TO for a goal, not get through. IE; Chapter 2 goal sends a goal once you REACH Chapter 2. Chapter 3 goal sends goal once you REACH Chapter 3. 

## Changelog
- Events in Chapter 2 should now also send checks
- Fixed some logic gates to help make spheres better
- Should be able to set a Chapter 3 goal with much less pain than before
- Region pathing has been updated, Blackrock Golem Cave Fire should now be correctly located within Chapter 1 checks
- Figured out how to make the Boar Boss a check, understand logic for doing other bosses, but still have to work on that. 
- Made more maps available for receiving items in. 

## Known Bugs
- Game may hardlock on you if you initiate a conversation or scene without having the required items. Attempts to just hardblock this have made things worse so far. So please **SAVE OFTEN**
- Certain maps may still not grant received items while on them. As always, Blackwitch Forest is your go-to safety to make sure you receive items
- May have some logic issues. Please let me know!
- You will duplicate the Blessings received. Ones received via the multiworld will not have the proper timer shown, but still work on said timer. The multiworld and story given Blessings operate separate of each other, so enjoy being OP for the time being. 
- Some items received have placeholder images. Even furtherstill, some of them don't function at all and just take up an inventory slot. Sorry 
- The game will still show you the vanilla item you were supposed to receive even if you did not receive said item. Please refer to the WitchSpring R Client for the item you received/sent.

0.1.3.description:
### Changelog

- Removed several items from the pool that would cause a softlock if the player attempted to use them
- Updated several location names to be more readable. EX: Event_9 - Item 1 ; Tutorial Ambush - Item 1

0.2.0.description:
## 0.2.0
### AI Disclaimer; Very reluctantly, this update was made with the help of AI. I do not plan to make this the norm, and I sat on this for a while if I even use this but there was just so much added and working that it would've been dumb not to use it. Further elaboration in discord

A large update over 0.1.3 — full chapter coverage, cross-platform/save reliability,
Universal Tracker support, many new checks, and a big pass of progression-logic fixes.

> **⚠️ Upgrade notes (read before updating)**
> - **Bridge data moved out of `%LOCALAPPDATA%`** into your game install folder. The client now asks for (or you set in `host.yaml`) your **WitchSpring R install path** on first use. See the README.
> - **Generate fresh seeds** with 0.2.0 — seeds made on 0.1.3 won't have the new checks or logic.

### Cross-platform & save reliability
- Bridge files now live in the **game install folder** (`<game>/Archipelago/`) instead of `%LOCALAPPDATA%`, so the mod works under **Steam Deck / Proton / macOS**.
- **Per-seed session folders** keep multiple seeds/saves cleanly separated — items and checks from one seed can never bleed into another.
- **Crash / Alt-F4 resilience:** items not yet written to your save are re-delivered on reload (save-marker reconcile); the delivery queue retries then skips a bad item instead of stalling; writes are atomic.
- **Items now deliver everywhere in the game.** The "safe to deliver" scene list previously only covered early areas, so items received in later chapters silently didn't arrive — it now covers the whole game (~200 scenes).

### Universal Tracker
- Added **Universal Tracker support** (generates without a yaml, reads slot data).

### World coverage & goals
- Playable range expanded; still largely untested beyond Chapter 3 but should function through Chapter 9.
- New regions: **RedBeard's Cave, Kanna's House, Luna's House, Ice Cave, Frozen Altar**.
- Removed the phantom "Chapter 8" (the game goes 7 → 9); the chapter-9 goal is now reachable.

### New location checks
- Around **two dozen new checks** across the game: event rewards (weapon-upgrade materials, key story items, etc.), **blessing checks** (Arua, Aimhard, Elysion, Durok), and **battle-reward checks** (Boar Captain, Shipwreck Cabin Key).
- Blessings are now sendable via a new in-game hook (they're granted differently from normal items, so they were previously unsendable).
- Missable / one-time checks are marked so the generator never hides required progression behind them.

### Progression-logic fixes
- **Mind Control Circle** is back in the pool and properly gates the content that needs it (Swamp, South Island, the Fire Cave branch, weapon-upgrade handout, and one entrance chest).
- **Reaching Chapter 2** now correctly requires the Fire Magic Spellbook **and** the Mind Control Circle; the Fire Spellbook itself sits behind Mind Control.
- **South Island** access now requires the Fire Magic Spellbook (you pass Black Joe on the way), so it can't be stranded behind that gate.
- **Reaching Chapter 3** now requires actually being able to reach the Lalaque Mine (Boar Captain's Tooth), not just holding the Mine Key.
- **Reaching Chapter 6** now requires the Prototype Steam Engine.
- **Ice Magic Spellbook** gates the Ice Cave and Durok Temple.
- **Laoba Mountain** (Warrior Camp) is available earlier — as soon as you've beaten the Boar Captain — instead of being locked to Chapter 3.
- Shipwreck progression modeled properly (Hold Key → Armory/Cabin Key → boss → Aimhard).
- Swim-only spots (a South Island islet, the Royal Pudding fight) now require the Aged Lalaque Berry (the swimming-pet item) so they aren't logically required before you can reach them.
- Numerous smaller rule corrections and a fixed Boar-Captain's-Tooth rule typo.

### Item pool
- **Removed craftable spell circles that soft-lock their quest** if received early (4-Orb Flame, 4-Pillar Ice) and the 3-Orb you already start with.
- Removed redundant / trap / non-functional items from the pool (e.g. Shipwreck Cannonball, Great Key, Matt's Garden Passage Key, a duplicate Red Gem, the phantom Chapter 8).
- Reclassified the Aged Lalaque Berry to progression (it now gates swim content) and Frozen Heart to filler.

### Plugin / quality
- New in-game **event blockers** (CheckSwitch hook) that gate story events safely without hard-locking.
- Cleaned up logging: routine per-event chatter is now hidden/debug-level, so the BepInEx console stays quiet unless something actually goes wrong.

0.3.0.description:
Adds in-game Archipelago messages and three optional "sanity" check categories
(Battlesanity, Bestiary, QuestSanity), plus logic and naming fixes.

> **⚠️ Upgrade notes (read before updating)**
> - **Both files must be updated together:** install the new `witchspringrap.apworld` **and** the new `WitchSpringRArchipelago.dll`. They are a matched pair.
> - **Generate fresh seeds** with 0.3.0 — the new options and checks won't exist on older seeds.
> - The new check categories are **optional toggles, off by default** — your existing yaml keeps working unchanged.

### In-game messages
- The game now shows **"Sent <item> to <player>"** when you trigger a check and **"Received <item> from <player>"** when an item is delivered, in the top-left info bar.

### New optional check categories (yaml toggles)
- **Please note that naming conventions and logic may not be fully proper for these settings as of yet**
- **Battlesanity** — a check for clearing each field battle (sends on win). ~136 checks.
- **Bestiary** — a check for defeating each enemy; any rank/shield variant counts. ~101 checks.
- **QuestSanity** — a check for completing each quest (main + request-board; sends when the quest is cleared, however it completes). ~140 checks.
- Each is a separate toggle (default **off**); the item pool auto-pads with filler so seeds stay fillable when they're enabled.

### Fixes
- **Region name:** "Blackhill Golem Cave" corrected to **"Backhill Golem Cave"**.
- **Arua Blessing** now also requires the Fire Magic Spellbook and Mind Control Circle (it isn't actually reachable until you can leave the starting area).
- Minor per-check chapter-gating fixes from playtesting.
0.3.0.title: v0.3.0

0.3.1.description: Forgot to save the basic logic to questsanity. Still not perfect, but better than before

0.3.2.description:
Updated logic for Battlesanity/Questsanity/Bestiary
Also added Livya, Justice and Demon of Greed who were previously missing from the list.

0.3.3.description:
Updated logic for Chapters 3+4 in regards to Bestiary/Questsanity/Battlesanity.

- Added some missing enemies from the Bestiary
- Removed 2 chests from Aimhards Temple - either I forgot where they are, or they're NG+ only, will review in more detail later.
- Pushed some battles/enemies into logic in a later chapter, even if they're already accessible just due to difficulty spike on them.
- Moved Volcano Road 3 and Royal Pudding to Chapter 5+ logic, since you gain access to them after the "To Elysion Temple" quest and I currently have no good way to track that in the logic documents. Safer bet this way. 
- Re-Added the South Island chest that was removed in the last update. Also renamed it.

0.3.4.description:
### GENERAL
- Finalized first pass at logic for Bestiary/Questsanity/Battlesanity for Chapters 1-6.(I accidentally set my yaml for a Chapter 7 goal instead of Chapter 9, that's on me, running a second pass now)
- Fixed a black-screen crash that could happen when multiple items were sent at once while you were changing scenes.
- Fixed the "Sent ____" popup from re-appearing every time you re-load the game. It never re-sent the checks, it was just the UI firing again.
- New in-game event gates. 
  - Added a fake "Narrel" item that you must receive from the multiworld for Narrel to move the boulder. 
  - Prototype Steam Engine and Matt's Garden Key are now correctly blocked from the vanilla item grant. 
  - Giving the Prototype Steam Engine to Kanna, *should* be gated and not allowed to happen until you receive the Prototype Steam Engine from the multiworld - **untested and I am working on this test**

### Battlesanity
- Numbers have been re-numbered based on the order that I fought them on the map. I tried to go from closest to entrance > farthest from entrance order.
- Added some Story/Event battles that were not previously logged.

### Bestiary
- Added more missing mobs from chapters 5+
- Fixed the logic on a bunch of them

### Disclaimer
I do **not** recommend setting a goal beyond Chapter 3(Goal: Chapter_4), because I have not had a chance to test the new Narrel block as of yet. I wanted to push this out early once I found out about the black-screen crash introduced in 0.3.3
